mitre_techniques_observed.description: Adversaries may enumerate communication between devices to map the ICS environment.
Adversaries may enumerate remote systems on the ICS network.
Adversaries may gather information about remote ICS systems — seen in Guardian AST attacks.
Adversaries may scan for open ports or services on ICS devices.
Adversaries may gather information about the current state of a industrial process.
Adversaries may send unauthorized commands to field controllers (Industroyer TTP).
Adversaries may identify control system tags and I/O points for targeting.
Adversaries may communicate over commonly used ICS ports (Modbus:502, IEC104:2404).
Adversaries may spoof reporting messages to mislead operators about system state.
Adversaries may transfer a new or modified program to a controller.
